reasoning: Attacked the whole derivation chain; it held, and it turns out not to be a chain at all. (1) RADIX: the equate block RBARON.MAC:462-508 sits in the .RADIX 16 region (set :74, first decimal island :6217), and the block's OWN decimal literals carry trailing periods (L.OBJ =28., DRNPNT =30., PF.QTX =512.*4) while S.MAXZ/S.DPTH/P.INDP/P.MNDP do not. So S.MAXZ = 0x19 = 25, S.DPTH = 0x100 = 256, P.INDP = 0x1080 = 4224, P.MNDP = 0x140 = 320. Both underlying radix readings verified independently. (2) The '(* 100)' on S.MAXZ is REAL and I proved it from code, not the comment: PSTSHL (:5213-5221) does `INC AX,SHELLS+5` — it increments the shell depth's MSB — then `CMP I,S.MAXZ&0FF` against 0x19. So the shell walks 0x0100 -> 0x1900 in 0x100 steps: 256 -> 6400. SHLMOT (:5192-5204) runs PSTSHL 4x/calc-frame, so 1024 depth-units/frame, 6 frames of flight. (3) SAME COORDINATE: COLSTP (:5774-5786) does `SBC AX,SHELLS+4` / `SBC AX,SHELLS+5` directly against the object depth — shell Z and plane depth are one 16-bit world coordinate, exactly as claimed. (4) ARITHMETIC REDONE: shell [256, 6400] vs plane [320, 4224]; 6400/4224 = 1.515. Holds. (5) OURS: guns.ts:133 depthToShellZ = depth * S_MAXZ / SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH, guns.ts:65 SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH = 800, enemy.ts:41/133 spawn depth P_INDP = 1080. Max shell z ~= S_MAXZ, so max reachable world depth = SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH = 800. The unreachable band is real.
corrections: THE FINDING UNDERSTATES ITS OWN INDEPENDENCE. It says 'Interacts with CB-011: even after S_MAXZ is corrected to 25 the mapping is still short.' In fact S_MAXZ CANCELS OUT of depthToShellZ entirely: reach = max_z * SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH / S_MAXZ = SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH for ANY value of S_MAXZ. Correcting S_MAXZ 19->25 changes the gun's reach by exactly ZERO. CB-012 has no dependency on CB-011 whatsoever; the sole governing constant is SHELL_RANGE_DEPTH. (This also refutes the premise that the finding's arithmetic collapses if a radix reading is wrong — both radix readings are right, and the defect would survive even if they were not, because both operands of the defect (800, 1080) come from OUR source.)

THE 74% FIGURE IS SLIGHTLY WRONG — it ignores WINDOW_Z. collides() (guns.ts:221-222) passes when |shell.z - depthToShellZ(depth)| <= WINDOW_Z = 1, so the true max hittable depth is (S_MAXZ + WINDOW_Z)/S_MAXZ * 800 = 20/19 * 800 ~= 842, not 800. Reach is ~78% of the 1080 spawn depth and the dead band is ~842..1080, not 800..1080. Defect real; magnitude marginally smaller than filed.

reasoning: I tried to kill this on the 'it's a fixed box in a different space' hypothesis and could not — it really does project. Traced the chain end to end. (a) CDSSET (:5527-5537): reads PLSTAT+4/+5 (PLANE DEPTH) into O.DPTH *and into DD.DML/DD.DMM — the Math Box DIVISOR registers* (declared :583-584 ';DIVISOR (LSB)/(MSB)'), then `LDX I,2 / JSR PLTEST` / `JSR MINMAX`. (b) PLTEST (:4984-5022, header ';ROTATE & PROJECT POINTS'): Z-axis rotation by PLSTAT+12/13 + PFROTN through TRIG, then a Y-axis or X-axis rotation by the plane's live attitude, then `JMP D.LOOP`. So the collision frame IS rotated by the live attitude. (c) D.LOOP — the load-bearing step, and it is NOT in RBARON.MAC, it is in RBGRND.MAC:624-656 (shipped). Its header is literally ';PROJECT X & Y BY Z'. Body: divisor = DB.TRP[Z] + O.DPTH (point Z + object depth), dividend = X -> `STA A,D.SZXD ;START DIVISION` -> ';SCALED X'; repeat for Y -> ';SCALED Y'. That is a genuine perspective divide by depth. RBARON's own call-site comments corroborate: ':1370 JSR D.LOOP ;SCALE BY DEPTH', ':5131 JSR D.LOOP ;PROJECT BY DEPTH'. (d) MINMAX (:5718-5769) then min/maxes those PROJECTED values into DB.TRP+0/+1 and +6/+7. (e) COLSTP (:5805-5816) tests the shell offset against DB.TRP+2/+3 (min) and DB.TRP+8/+9 (max). The window's divisor is the plane depth, so it shrinks as ~1/depth. Ours (guns.ts:68,70,222): WINDOW_X = WINDOW_Y = 32, depth-invariant. Structural divergence is real and is not tunable by a constant, exactly as claimed.
corrections: '.COLLD = 0C = 12 bytes' for a 4-point frame is arithmetically impossible and is an undecoded operand. DB.TRP points are 6 bytes (RBGRND.MAC:661 ';Z LSB,Z MSB,X LSB,X MSB,Y LSB,Y MSB'), so 4 points = 24 bytes. The 4-POINT count is nevertheless CORRECT and I verified it from the loop, not the equate: MINMAX enters at `LDX I,.COLLD-3*2+4` = ((0C-3)*2)+4 = 22 (MACRO-65 is strict left-to-right, no precedence) and its inner loop steps down by 6 — 22, 16, 10, 4 — i.e. the Y words of points 3,2,1,0; the outer loop then DEX DEX to cover the X axis. Four points, two axes. The '12 bytes' gloss is wrong but immaterial.

Scope: only the X/Y box is perspective-scaled. COLSTP's Z gate (:5784-5786, `CPY I,80 / SBC I,0`) is a FIXED +/-0x80 window in unprojected world depth. The finding's aside that 'the Z half of the test IS correct' is consistent with this, but the fix must NOT accidentally project the Z bound too.

reasoning: Radix region proven, not assumed. RBARON.MAC:481 `EX.ACY =-20` sits between .RADIX 16 (:74) and the first decimal island (:6217), and the SAME equate block spells its decimal literals with trailing periods (L.OBJ =28. :462, DRNPNT =30. :508, PF.QTX =512.*4 :452). No period on EX.ACY => -0x20 = -32. INDEPENDENT PROOF that this specific block is hex: .EXPL2 =12 (:480) must be 0x12 = 18 for UPPLEX to work — PLSTAT+7 counts up, `CMP I,.EXPL1` (6) flips fall->explode and `CMP I,.EXPL2` ends it (:2970, :2983); 18 total minus 6 falling = the 12 exploding frames the comment names, and OUR OWN explosion.ts:16 already documents an 18-frame total. If that block were decimal, our own working model would be impossible. Consumption verified: UPPLEX (:2961-2967) is a true 16-bit add of EX.ACY into PLSTAT+0E/+0F, unconditionally at the head of the routine before any branch. Ours (explosion.ts:33) is `export const EX_ACY = -20`, applied in stepWreck as `wreck.vy + EX_ACY` every live frame (explosion.ts:102). ARITHMETIC REDONE: sum 1..6 = 21; ROM 32*21 = 672, ours 20*21 = 420; 420/672 = 0.625, so ours is 62.5% of the ROM = 37.5% short. Auditor's numbers check out exactly.
corrections: The REASONING contains one non-sequitur that should not be repeated to a developer: 'UPPLEX confirms the 16-bit interpretation directly ... i.e. it adds the 16-bit constant -0x0020'. The `ADC I,EX.ACY` / `ADC I,EX.ACY&0FF00/100` pair proves only that EX.ACY is a 16-bit SIGNED constant — it is byte-identical in form whether the value is -0x20 (0xFFE0) or decimal -20 (0xFFEC). What actually pins the value is the radix region plus the trailing-period convention (and the .EXPL2 cross-check above). Conclusion (-32) is right; that particular argument for it is not.

reasoning: This is the one I most expected to kill — 67.5 deg requires an angle-encoding assumption the auditor inferred from a single clamp comment. I derived the encoding independently from the trig code and the auditor lands exactly right. (1) THE ADD: UPPLX0 (:2997-3003) `LDA PLSTAT+12 / ADC I,80 / STA PLSTAT+12 / LDA PLSTAT+13 ;MSB / ADC I,1 / STA PLSTAT+13` — a 16-bit add of 0x0180 = 384 to the wreck's Z angle, once per calc frame. The paired `ADC I,1` is exactly the undecoded operand the auditor flags. (2) PLSTAT+12/13 IS an angle: PLTEST (:4986-4991) feeds it (+ PFROTN) straight into TRIG. (3) THE UNITS — proven from TRIG (:5984-5990), not from a comment: given the 16-bit angle V (A=MSB, Y=LSB), TRIG computes `LSR / AND I,3` => quadrant = (V>>9)&3, and `TYA / ROR / LSR` => in-quadrant angle = (V>>2)&0x7F, which is handed to T.SINE whose own header (:6017) says ';ACC = ANGLE (0 TO 7F)' and which treats 0x80 as 90 deg (:6008-6009, `LDA I,080 / SBC TEMP1` for the cosine). Therefore one quadrant = 0x80 units of in-quad angle = 0x200 = 512 raw units = 90 deg, and a full turn = 2048 raw units. P.MAXR = 0x1FF = 511 ';90 DEGREE MAX ROTATION' (:471) corroborates to the unit. (4) ARITHMETIC: 384/2048 = 0.1875 turn = 67.5 deg = 3*pi/8 rad. Revolution in 360/67.5 = 5.33 frames. Ours (explosion.ts:51) SPIN_RATE = Math.PI/4 = 45 deg => 8 frames/rev, 2/3 the ROM rate. Every number in the finding is correct.
corrections: The finding's derivation of the angle scale rests solely on P.MAXR's comment ('0x200 = 512 units = 90 deg'), which is an inference from a clamp constant, not a proof. It happens to be right, but a developer should be pointed at the actual proof, which is TRIG's bit-shifts (RBARON.MAC:5984-5990) + T.SINE's ';ACC = ANGLE (0 TO 7F)' header (:6017): quadrant = (V>>9)&3 and in-quad angle = (V>>2)&0x7F, hence 512 raw units per quadrant and 2048 per turn. Cite that, not P.MAXR alone.

reasoning: The core claim — 'lives.ts asserts the durations are not ROM-pinned; they are' — is true, and the quote is accurate: lives.ts:29-31 literally reads 'the three death sub-stages have NO ROM-pinned durations (unlike WO.CNT=5) ... No fabricated durations.' RADIX: .TIME1 =10 (:505) and .TIME2 =1C (:506) are in the same proven-hex equate block, no trailing periods => 16 and 28 decimal. CLOCK CHECKED (the brief's trap): EOLSEQ is called from `JSR EOLSEQ` at RBARON.MAC:825, which is inside MAIN (:763-878) — the calc-frame loop, NOT the 250 Hz NMI handler that carries MODSND/SCOREM/PLPROP. So the 96 ms step applies. ARITHMETIC REDONE: 16 * 96 ms = 1.536 s; 28 * 96 ms = 2.688 s; 12 * 96 ms = 1.152 s. All three of the auditor's conversions are correct. Mechanism verified: EOGTMR is INC'd once per call (:1115); ENDLFE fires on `CMP I,.TIME2 / BCC 45$ / JMP ENDLFE` (:1124-1126); the starfield fires on `CPX I,.TIME1 ;TIME FOR STARFIELD ?` (:1162-1163). The durations are pinned. Our lives.ts has no timing at all (DEATH_SEQUENCE is a bare 3-element cursor, advanceDeath just increments phase).
corrections: THE STAGE BREAKDOWN IS FABRICATED. The finding says a shot-down death is 'bullet-hole/spiral for frames 0-15, starfield for frames 16-27'. The bullet-hole is NOT part of the EOL timeline at all. B.HOLE's 0->0x0C stepping (:1101-1104) lives on the RETURNING-ACE branch (reached only via `BIT GMEND1 / BMI 30$` FALLING THROUGH at :1057-1058, i.e. only while EOL has NOT started, and then only when GMEND0 bit0 is set at :1098-1100). Once GMEND1 bit7 is set — which is the first thing a shell death does (:1061-1067) — EOLSEQ branches straight to 30$ and NEVER touches B.HOLE again. So B.HOLE is a pre-EOL, returning-ace-channel counter, not the first 16 frames of the shot-down sequence. What .TIME1 = 16 actually marks is the STARFIELD ONSET; what precedes it is the spiral/PF-scroll, not a bullet-hole animation.

Off-by-one in the frame labelling (harmless to the totals, but do not hard-code it): .TIME1 is compared against the POST-increment EOGTMR (INC at :1115, `LDX EOGTMR` at :1162), while .TIME2 is compared against the PRE-increment value (LDA at :1114, CMP at :1124). So the shell channel actually runs EOGTMR = 0..28 (29 EOLSEQ calls) and the starfield trips when the incremented counter reaches 16. '28 frames / 2.688 s' is right to within one frame; the exact stage boundaries are not what the finding states.

reasoning: I attacked the branch polarity hardest — BVC/BVS on a BIT is the classic place to get a two-channel claim exactly backwards — and the auditor read it correctly. EOLSEQ:1059-1067: `BIT GREND` (N=bit7, V=bit6) / `BPL 10$` / `LDA I,0` / `LDX I,0C0` / `BVC 5$` — BVC branches when bit6 is CLEAR, i.e. the SHELL death (GREND = 0x80), taking A=0, X=0xC0 => EOGTMR = 0, GMEND1 = 0xC0. Bit6 SET (ground) falls through to `LDA I,0F` / `LDX I,0A0` => EOGTMR = 0x0F = 15, GMEND1 = 0xA0. Exactly as filed. Both GREND writers verified: :3758-3759 `LDA I,80 / STA GREND ;SHELL CD`; :4643-4645 `LDA SAVX+1 / ORA I,0C0 / STA GREND ;D6=GROUND COLLISION`. Durations: ENDLFE at pre-inc EOGTMR >= 28 (:1124-1126), so the ground channel runs 15..27 = 13 frames = 1.248 s vs the shell channel's 28 = 2.688 s. Third differentiator verified at MAIN:783-784 `BIT GREND / BVS 20$ ;PLAYER RAN INTO GROUND` — this skips PFMOTN, NWPLNE and PLMOTN, so the world genuinely freezes on a ground crash and not on a shoot-down. Mountain-crash scene verified at :1128-1137 (SCENE1/SCENE2). OURS: lives.ts has one DEATH_SEQUENCE and beginDeath(side) records only ENSIDE; a grep of src/ for GREND / ground-collision / death-channel / shotDown returns NOTHING. Divergence real.
corrections: 'GREND = 0xC0' for the ground crash is an under-decoded operand. :4643-4645 is `LDA SAVX+1 ;MOUNTAIN OFFSET OF CRASH / ORA I,0C0 / STA GREND` — GREND is (mountain_offset | 0xC0), and EOLSEQ reads the payload straight back out at :1130-1132 (`LDA GREND / AND I,3F / TAX`) to pick which mountain to draw. Bits 7+6 are the channel discriminator (that part is right); the low 6 bits are NOT zero and carry the crash site. A clone that models GREND as the literal byte 0xC0 loses the mountain index.

The finding UNDERSTATES the divergence — there is a fourth differentiator it did not claim. The `LDA I,40 / ORA GMEND1` that sets D6 = ';(D6=PF EOL FLAG)' (horizon scroll + playfield rotation, :1120-1122) runs ONLY on the EOGTMR == 0 frame (:1117 `BNE 40$`). The shell channel is seeded at 0 and hits it; the ground channel is seeded at 15 and NEVER DOES — which is also why its GMEND1 seed is 0xA0 (D6 clear) rather than 0xC0 (D6 already set). So the ground crash gets no PF spiral/horizon-scroll either. Four differences, not three.

reasoning: THE MECHANIC IS ALREADY IMPLEMENTED, CORRECTLY, IN OUR CODE. The finding is filed as NO_COUNTERPART and states 'Nothing in lives.ts models any of this' and — the load-bearing sentence — 'there is no line in our combat spine that even attempts the check.' That second statement is false. src/core/returning-ace.ts:113-126 `evadeCheck(ace, turnRate, roll)` implements EVERY element the finding lists as missing, element for element: (1) `if (ace.firstPass) return 'evaded'` and consume it => BEFLAG ';FIRST TIME FREE' (:1088-1093); (2) `Math.sign(turnRate) === ace.side` => `LDA ENSIDE / EOR PLDELX / BPL 20$ ;SORRY WRONG X` (:1081-1083); (3) `Math.abs(turnRate) >= HARD_TURN` with `export const HARD_TURN = 0x1c` (returning-ace.ts:46) => `JSR SPABS / CMP I,1C / BCC 20$ ;PLAYER DIES` (:1084-1087) — and note our constant is written 0x1c, i.e. our code ALREADY read the radix correctly, which is the very thing the finding implies we got wrong; (4) `roll < 0.5 ? 'evaded' : 'hit'` => `JSR RANDOM / BMI 20$ ;AFTER - ONLY 50/50` (:1090-1091). returning-ace.ts:17-27 even quotes the ROM branch comments verbatim. The finding is SELF-REFUTING in its own text: 'Whether it belongs in lives.ts or in returning-ace.ts (another pair's file) is a design call' — the auditor named the exact file that contains the code and filed NO_COUNTERPART without opening it. Acting on this finding as written would have a developer build a SECOND copy of a mechanic that already exists, with a fabricated per-game BEFLAG. It is also the case that pairs 1 and 3 filed overlapping claims (CD-010, EN-019); three agents agreeing is not evidence, and all three missed the same file. Verdict: REFUTED as filed. (Residue, for a correctly-scoped NEW finding: `evadeCheck` has NO callers outside its own file — grep of src/ for evadeCheck/beginPass/HARD_TURN returns nothing — so the mechanic is implemented but UNWIRED, and the ROM's one-frame trigger gate `PLSTAT+7 == 0x0C` (:1078-1080) is absent. That is a WIRING gap of class DIVERGENCE/DEAD_CODE, sized s-m, not a missing mechanic of class NO_COUNTERPART sized m.)
corrections: Bonus radix/scope kill, in case any of this is re-filed: 'one free escape per game' is WRONG. BEFLAG (RBARON.MAC:128) has exactly three references in the entire quarry — the declaration, the `BIT BEFLAG` read (:1088), and the `LDA I,-1 / STA BEFLAG` set (:1093). It is NEVER cleared. GMINIT's INITIAL block zeroes only the range [PLDELX(:164) .. MESTM2(:182)] (`LDX I,MESTM2-PLDELX / STA ZX,PLDELX`, :1237-1240), and BEFLAG at :128 sits BELOW PLDELX and is therefore outside that range. So the free dodge is once per RAM-clear / power-on, NOT once per game. Our returning-ace.ts models `firstPass` per-ace-pass, which is a third thing again — but the finding's proposed 'per-game BEFLAG' is unsupported by the source.
